dialogos.texto_en: Yo, what are you looking at?
What's with that expression?
Why are you here?\nYou probably have other places to go.
You also told me where you're going.\nIn that case, the conditions should be equal, right?
...I suppose they are.\nWell, I'm just looking at souvenirs,\nso please feel free to do as you like.
Fine then.
(I was thinking of confirming what her proposal\n really meant, but it doesn't seem like\n she's interested in talking to me.)
...Hey, Itsuki.\nCan I ask you something?
Wh-what is it?!
What kind of store is this?\nEveryone is lining up for hand towels or something.
This is a specialty store for small fabric items.
Oh wow, that kinda store exists, eh?\nThere's something Okinawan\nabout all of these color schemes and designs.
These are Okinawan traditional dyeing techniques\ncalled 'bingata.'\nApparently, they're original products of this store.
They're beautiful. But, would people really buy\nand use hand towels like this? I can understand\nif they were bath towels or handkerchiefs.
There are many uses for them, you know?\nYou can use them as a book cover\nor attach handles to make them into a bag.
With a design like this, you could even frame it\nand use it as interior decoration.
Don't you think it would give off\nan Okinawan atmosphere in your room?
Oh... I thought it was just something to wipe your\nhands or sweat with, but it seems like there are many\ndifferent uses for it, depending on your perspective.
You're quite knowledgeable, Uesugi-kun,\nbut you seem to be a bit lacking in this area.
I didn't use to be interested\nin anything that wasn't related to my grades.
And now?
...Being with you guys,\nI seem to be gaining a lot of useless knowledge.
It's not useless knowledge.\nIt's knowledge that enriches your heart and mind.
So, using this hand towel for various purposes\nis one of those knowledge tokens, eh. Well,\nI couldn't think of anything except for wiping my hands.
You don't need to think too hard about it.
Let's see.\nFor example, you could use it to wrap a present,\ncouldn't you?
I see.\nI could probably do that much myself.
Ah... If I wrap Okinawan sweets\nwith this hand towel, it would make\na perfect souvenir for Raiha-chan, wouldn't it.
That's a great idea.\nAlright, let's start by choosing the hand towel first!
Huh?
Since Raiha likes penguins,\nmaybe a design like that would be%p-2;――
Wa-wait a minute, Uesugi-kun!\nDon't steal my idea!
Come on, don't be so stingy.
Alright, I'll choose the hand towel.\nAnd how about you choose the snacks to wrap inside?
Please help me out for Raiha's sake.
...Well, I guess there's no other choice.\nLet's compromise on that.
You have good sense.\nA little idea can make things feel more special.
Heheh!\nI think so too.
It's more stylish than anything else.\nRaiha will definitely be happy.
I'll be happy too, if she likes it.
I'd like to do the same, but...\nUsing the same pattern seems a bit lackluster.
Then how about I choose the snacks\nand you choose the hand towel?
Thanks.
Eh?
I mean, I'm happy\nyou're considering Raiha's feelings, too.
No, it's nothing, really.\nIt's just something I like doing.
I don't really think about that kinda stuff.\nI have to choose a souvenir\nthat she'll be happy with.
...In that case, please choose the hand towel.
Huh?\nThen what about you?
I'll choose the snacks to wrap inside. When it comes\nto choosing delicious things, I'm more suited\nfor the job than you, Uesugi-kun. Right?
Did you find a good hand towel?
What about this one?\nThe blue color looks like the sea in Okinawa,\ndoesn't it? It's really nice.
That pattern looks beautiful\nfolded or spread out, which is nice.
Okay, I'll go with this one.
Now we just need to choose the sweets.
I'm glad I had you with me, Itsuki.\nI wouldn't have come up\nwith something like this on my own.
It's for Raiha-chan's sake.
Our souvenir will be from the two of us, right?\nI'm sure Raiha will be happy.
Wait, from the two of us?
That's right, isn't it?\nWe're both choosing Raiha's souvenir.
That's a little, awkward...
Why? Is it bad if we both buy Raiha a souvenir?
No, it's not that.
Anyway, let's forget about this idea.\nI'll think of something else, so excuse me.
What?\nHey, wait a minute!
(What's going on? So sudden...)
